data.title: Write RED test for hook-error-handler.sh: registration + enriched JSONL output
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## What to Build
Create `tests/hooks/test-hook-error-handler.sh` with behavioral tests for the shared ERR handler:

1. `test_register_hook_err_handler_sets_trap`: Source handler, call `_dso_register_hook_err_handler "test-hook.sh"` — verify the ERR trap is registered (trap output includes `_dso_hook_err_handler`)
2. `test_err_trigger_writes_enriched_jsonl`: Register handler with `_DSO_HOOK_NAME="test-hook.sh"`, trigger `false`, verify JSONL written to `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl` with all 7 fields: ts, hook, line, repo_root, plugin_version, bash_version, os
3. `test_handler_exits_0_on_err`: Verify that triggering ERR with registered handler exits 0 (fail-open)
4. `test_mkdir_created_lazily`: Verify handler creates `~/.claude/logs/` on first write

Test harness must use isolated `TEST_HOME` (mktemp -d + `export HOME="$TEST_HOME"`) — never write to real `$HOME/.claude/logs/`.

Update `.test-index` with RED marker:
```
plugins/dso/hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh:[test_register_hook_err_handler_sets_trap]
```

Test file naming: `test-hook-error-handler.sh` → normalizes to `testhookerrorhandlersh`. Source normalizes to `hookerrorhandlersh`. Substring match ✓

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/hooks/test-hook-error-handler.sh` exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/hooks/test-hook-error-handler.sh
- [ ] Tests FAIL (RED) before hook-error-handler.sh exists — T2 makes them GREEN
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-hook-error-handler.sh 2>/dev/null; [ $? -ne 0 ]
- [ ] `.test-index` updated with RED marker for hook-error-handler.sh
  Verify: grep '\[test_register_hook_err_handler_sets_trap\]' .test-index
- [ ] `.test-index` maps source file to test file
  Verify: grep 'hook-error-handler.sh' .test-index
- [ ] Tests use isolated TEST_HOME (no writes to real $HOME/.claude/)
  Verify: grep -q 'TEST_HOME\|mktemp' tests/hooks/test-hook-error-handler.sh
